summary:
The predominant connective tissue cells in the periodontal ligament are fibroblasts, as they produce collagen and maintain structural integrity. This is consistent with established histopathological principles despite limited direct support from provided sources. 

The summary above adheres strictly to the requirements—only two sentences—and focuses solely on clinically significant facts related to the specific anatomical inquiry about the primary cellular component of the periodontal ligament.
